_meta.null_signal_reason: EU AI Act transparency code and OpenAI capability escalation provide material change.
_meta.cycle_disposition_reason: EU Code of Practice on AI-generated content transparency is nearing finalization and OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 with agentic capabilities, both representing material changes in AI governance and capability.

lead_signal.headline: EU finalizes Code of Practice on transparency for AI-generated content ahead of August 2026 AI Act obligations.
lead_signal.body: The European Commission is finalizing a voluntary Code of Practice on Transparency of AI-Generated Content that will guide providers and deployers on marking, detecting, and labelling AI-generated and manipulated content, including deepfakes and AI-generated text on matters of public interest. This code is expected to become a key reference for regulators and courts when assessing compliance with Article 50 of the EU AI Act, which enters into force on 2 August 2026.

module_0.title: EU finalizes Code of Practice on transparency for AI-generated content ahead of August 2026 AI Act obligations.
module_0.body: The European Commission is finalizing a voluntary Code of Practice on Transparency of AI-Generated Content that will guide providers and deployers on marking, detecting, and labelling AI-generated and manipulated content, including deepfakes and AI-generated text on matters of public interest. This code is expected to become a key reference for regulators and courts when assessing compliance with Article 50 of the EU AI Act, which enters into force on 2 August 2026. The code introduces a two-layered marking approach involving secured metadata and watermarking, and optional fingerprinting and logging, to ensure that AI-generated content is detectable and traceable. For deployers, the code requires clear disclosure of deepfakes and AI-generated text on matters of public interest, with a common icon and taxonomy to distinguish fully AI-generated from AI-assisted content. This development signals a tightening of transparency requirements for generative AI systems in the EU, with potential implications for global content platforms and media organizations that operate in the European market.

governance_health_composite.note: No material change in governance health this week.
